items.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

children.content: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia’s far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

items.title: Serious crash involving road train closes Stuart Highway in far north SA

children.content: A major outback highway in South Australia's north has been closed in both directions after a crash involving a road train and a vehicle early on Tuesday morning.

image.altText: Road signage on the side of a highway intersection in the Northern Territory.

items.title: SA Police investigating theft of organ pipes from heritage-listed former church

children.content: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

image.altText: An old musical organ has gold coloured piping and rich dark coloured wood

items.title: Factory workers reunite 16 years after closure of major food manufacturer

children.content: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

image.altText: About a dozen women working on a production line in a factory.

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

gallery.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

thumbnail.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

items.title: 'We're taking him home': Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated

children.content: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

image.altText: The start of a repatriation process at the SA Museum.

items.title: Truro businesses call for certainty on freight bypass project

children.content: SA businesses situated along a major thoroughfare say they are holding back on making decisions about their future, amid uncertainty surrounding a slated road project that could divert traffic around the area.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

items.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint

children.content: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

image.altText: A woman speaking to a dog standing on its back legs at a cafe serving window

items.title: Amid push to rename Grey, SA's largest federal seat, who was its namesake?

children.content: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?

image.altText: A collage showing a historic portrait photo in black and white with a map of the seat of Grey and a painting from the NZ wars

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: Australian astronaut offered place on breakthrough space flight

children.content: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.

image.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.

items.title: Vegemite banned, Tim Tams welcome: Baseballer brings Aussie flavour to US

children.content: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

image.altText: Curtis Mead on the field during a baseball game.

items.title: Gold medallist who endured 16 surgeries in 9 months hangs up oars

children.content: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.

image.altText: woman holds a golf medal

items.title: Autumn rain in SA brings 'incredible' boom in birdlife

children.content: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.

image.altText: A pelican swims in a river and is reflected in the water.

items.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament

children.content: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

items.title: Plagued by 18 months of blackouts, locals say $10m fix is long overdue

children.content: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.

image.altText: A man in a grey hoodie standing behind beer taps.

items.title: Drink driver crashes into pole, over embankment but escapes uninjured, police say

children.content: SA Police say a woman has lost her licence after crashing into a timber pole and onto a beach in Adelaide while drink driving.

image.altText: Grey mazda sedan with crashed front on beach with timber post hanging from front window.

items.title: 'Intolerable': When $786m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

gallery.altText: A photo showing the length of the new Bridgewater Bridge across the RIver Derwent with hills in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A photo showing the length of the new Bridgewater Bridge across the RIver Derwent with hills in the background.

items.title: NT diphtheria cases declining as 10,000 immunised, one death confirmed

children.content: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Inside Xi's banquet diplomacy and why Albanese missed out on Peking duck

children.content: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.

image.altText: A man raises a glass of champagne from behind a lectern.

images.altText: Officers usher away a protester in parliament house.

articles.title: Israeli politician calls for investigation after Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse

children.content: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into the alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, which was intercepted by the Israeli military in the Mediterranean last week.

image.altText: Yair Lapid speaks at a lectern gesturing with one hand.

articles.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

children.content: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

articles.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan

children.content: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.

image.altText: A woman in a blue jacket looking pensive.

items.title: 170m chimneys come crashing down as power station demolition begins

children.content: The demolition of the Liddell Power Station, which powered homes and businesses in NSW for more than 50 years, is underway.

image.altText: Power station chimneys demolished

items.title: 'Catastrophic failure' after drones malfunction at Vivid Sydney show

children.content: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7.30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

image.altText: A man, wearing a tshirt and an akubra hat, stands outside in front of trees.

items.title: The many phases of Miles Davis, who would have turned 100 today

children.content: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

items.title: Using AI to manage your budget? This is what experts want you to know

image.altText: The hands hands of a woman typing, while she's reclined on the sofa.

items.title: Richard Gadd 'couldn't escape' the story he wanted to tell after Baby Reindeer

children.content: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

image.altText: A TV still of Richard Gadd, 37, with a beard and short back and sides, in a leather jacket, eyeballing the camera.

items.title: Missing man's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'

children.content: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.

items.title: 'My time to go': The words that showed Daniher's humanity in tragedy

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

items.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

image.altText: Kristian Gkolomeev,holds up two trophies
